synth_clean.py.justification: Exemplary readability with type aliases, docstrings, and named pure functions; elegant two-candidate path generation avoids brute force, clean memoized recursion, and both parts handled via a proper main().

tomek.py.justification: Strong domain modelling with a PadType enum, descriptive names, an explanatory docstring, and scalable iterative memoization, marred only by some repetition, a hard external dependency, and generic Exception use.

online_2.py.justification: Clean memoized class-based solution with reasonable naming, but reinvents itertools.product, uses a mutable default arg, duplicates setup across part1/part2 via side-effecting instance attributes, and packs dense gap logic into parse_moves.

synth_mid.py.justification: Readable, well-commented, and well-named, but relies on permutation brute force and hardcodes three expansion levels with no memoization, so it only handles Part 1 and would not scale.

synth_primitive.py.justification: Cryptic single-letter names, no whitespace, a fragile order-picking heuristic, a gap check that fails to break, and Part-1-only scope with no memoization make it dense and hard to follow.

online_1.py.justification: Severely over-engineered: hundreds of lines of unrelated animation/rendering code wrap a solver built on global mutable state and a confusing dual-mode (length-vs-string) design with near-duplicate functions and cryptic names.
